pages.raw_text:
One more change was made in
the grade separation plans of the
Northern Pacific by the council
committee last night. The rail-
road had adopted City Engineer
Ralston's suggestion for three tiers
of posts, in the center and along
the sidewalk lines, but the council
did not approve of this after de-
liberation last night and voted to
adopt system of stronger Orders
and only one line of posts, down
the center, of the Washington. Wall
Howard and Division ST. subways.


Mr.. Ralston's advise on the prob
lem was asked and he informed the
council the scheme was all right If
the railroad would be compelled to
use larger giriers. Councilmen
Estep and Lambert will take up the
matter of closing Third av. private
ly with interested property holders
and report at the next council
meeting.


Albert Held again demanded re-
consideration of the opening of
Bernard St, and he and several
